title: Iran Formally Rejects Trump's 15-Point Plan, Issues 5-Point Counterproposal
detail: Iran formally rejected Trump's 15-point ceasefire plan, calling it 'maximalist and unreasonable' via state-run Fars news agency and Al Jazeera. Foreign Minister Araghchi stated 'We do not want a ceasefire,' arguing it would lead to a vicious cycle of repeated war. Iran issued its own 5-point counterproposal: (1) halt to US-Israeli aggression and assassinations; (2) mechanisms ensuring war does not resume; (3) payment of war reparations; (4) end to attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and Iranian-backed militias in Iraq; (5) international recognition of Iran's authority over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's military spokesperson mocked Washington's offer.

title: IDF Strikes Tehran Military Industries and Cruise Missile Facilities
detail: The IDF conducted overnight strikes against military industries and sites producing cruise missiles in Tehran, as well as weapons production facilities, missile manufacturing sites, and air defense systems. IDF also struck bridges to prevent weapons transfers southward into Lebanon, and targeted financial entities and infrastructure serving Hezbollah. IDF sources stated approximately 70% of designated operation targets have been struck.

title: Hezbollah Fires 100+ Rockets at IDF Positions in South Lebanon
detail: Hezbollah said its fighters targeted Israeli troops 'massed in the border towns of Naqura and Qawzah' and across other border sites with more than 100 rockets. Israel continued strikes on south Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs. The IDF reported approximately 700 Hezbollah fighters have been eliminated since the start of operations. Israeli strikes also targeted Lebanon bridges to halt Hezbollah resupply.

title: US Temporarily Lifts Sanctions on Iranian Oil to Ease Energy Crisis
detail: The US announced a temporary 30-day lifting of sanctions on the sale and delivery of Iranian oil already loaded onto tankers by March 20, which must be unloaded by April 19. The measure is intended to release tens of millions of barrels of already-purchased oil onto the global market to ease energy price pressure and buy Washington more time to achieve war aims. At current prices, the amount of oil involved is worth over $14 billion for Tehran. Energy experts warned the measure is unlikely to make a material difference to the broader energy crisis. IEA head described the situation as the 'greatest global energy security challenge in history.'

title: Senior Houthi Official Signals Naval Blockade and Red Sea Attacks Imminent
detail: Senior Houthi politburo member Mohammed al-Bukhaiti declared 'All options at the military level are possible' and stated the group was considering a naval blockade targeting vessels of 'aggressor countries' in the Red Sea. Two unnamed Houthi leaders told the AP the group intends to resume missile and drone attacks on ships. The Houthis retain a significant drone stockpile despite depleted missile reserves from the Gaza war era, and have reinforced positions along the Red Sea coast including Hodeidah.
